title: Memetic Portability

content.question: Can you describe your business in 8 words or less without losing what makes it matter?
content.equation: MP = (Retell Accuracy × Share Rate × Platform Spread) ÷ Complexity
sop: Write your full pitch, then cut it in half
Cut it in half again until it's under 8 words
Say it out loud to 3 people and see if they can repeat it back correctly
Use the version that survived unprompted repetition
content.explainer: Complexity is the enemy of distribution. Every extra word halves the chance someone repeats it.
questions: Can you describe your business in 8 words or less without losing what makes it matter?
If someone had to explain your business to a friend in one text message, what would they write?
What's the shortest version of your pitch that still makes someone want to know more?
content.equation_example: Retell Accuracy 0.80 (80%), Share Rate 0.20 (20%), Platform Spread 3, Complexity 2. MP = (0.80 × 0.20 × 3) ÷ 2 = 0.24. Track weekly: higher means the idea survives retelling and jumps platforms; lower means it distorts or stalls.
content.closing_statement: If it can't survive being repeated, it won't spread.

caption:
Principle Nº 5: Memetic Portability

Ideas that compress survive. Complexity is the enemy of distribution.

Ask yourself: Can you describe your business in 8 words or less without losing what makes it matter?

Follow @TheGoldenSocietyClub for a new principle every day.
